text: SUPERSEDED by qgjmjh (2026-07-08). Historical: this recorded the fork-sync target of 0 commits behind upstream/main and X ahead with TLH deltas rebased/reapplied onto upstream/main. Diego rejected that perpetual rebase-on-top model in favor of a non-rebase, release/cluster intake workflow — see qgjmjh and docs/UPSTREAM-SYNC.md. Do not reintroduce the rebase-on-top or '0 behind' target.

TLH runtime spawning is a product requirement for this fork: child subagents must prefer the resolved parent/private Pi runtime (for example the private The Last Harness runtime) over ambient PATH. If a runtime/package root is found but unusable, fail clearly rather than falling through to Homebrew/global pi; tests should adapt their harnesses rather than weakening production spawn precedence.

When PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR is set, it is the active TLH profile root and should isolate user agents, chains, settings, skills, packages, and configured directories from legacy ~/.agents discovery. Do not reintroduce implicit legacy-profile scanning just to match upstream; cross-profile leakage is a fork compatibility bug.

Agent and chain discovery in the fork intentionally preserves TLH deltas on top of upstream package discovery: configured agent dirs remain supported, user/profile-owned agents take precedence over configured/extra/package entries, package-provided agents/chains should be discoverable from nested cwd package roots, and .pi/.agents project-root markers keep precedence over package-manifest fallback.

Intercom bridge discovery should prefer explicit/env/runtime-configured sources first, then package/settings/TLH locations including npm and tmp extension installs; legacy extension paths are fallback-only. This preserves TLH package/runtime installs without letting old global extension locations unexpectedly override them.

The fork's completion/control guard behavior balances implementation safety with advisory-agent ergonomics: implementation agents should fail when they claim completion without a real mutation attempt, VCS and gh PR mutations count as mutations, but read-only/advisory agents such as oracle/librarian/web-scout should not be falsely flagged for thinking-heavy or future-tense analysis.

Subagent model selection should inherit the current parent session model when no explicit child model is configured, including foreground, parallel, chain, async, and revived resume runs. Explicit execution or agent model overrides remain authoritative; per-dispatch fallback models run before agent fallbacks, and fallback notices should only appear when an actual retry uses them.

Output override handling should make task-level output instructions authoritative while preserving compatibility with legacy detection patterns. Agent-written output files should not be clobbered by final receipts, false/'false' output values disable output, and file-only mode should return concise saved-output references rather than full content.

The TLH fork intentionally removes human-facing direct subagent slash commands and bundled prompt shortcuts such as /run, /chain, /parallel, /run-chain, /parallel-review, and /review-loop. Supported launch surfaces are natural-language delegation, the subagent(...) tool, and narrowly useful diagnostics/status commands; do not reintroduce broad slash workflow syntax just because upstream adds slash features.

TLH intentionally diverges from upstream profile-load behavior for subagent settings. Upstream applySubagentProfile currently replaces settings.subagents with profile.subagents, which is acceptable when profiles own the whole subagents object but is unsafe for TLH because TLH installer/profile state owns sibling fields such as subagents.agentDirs, disableBuiltins, and disableThinking. Losing agentDirs removes TLH bundled minor agents from discovery while pi-subagents still appears to work with builtins only. The TLH fork should preserve existing sibling subagents.* fields and replace only subagents.agentOverrides when applying a saved profile; this is a deliberate TLH compatibility delta, not accidental upstream drift.

Diego wants the TLH pi-subagents fork distributed like the other TLH fork packages: publish a Diego-scoped npm package (e.g. @diegopetrucci/pi-subagents) and switch TLH from git commit pins to the npm package once the scoped package contains the fork fixes.

Diego explicitly rejected the perpetual rebase-on-top upstream-sync model for this fork (the old '0 behind / X ahead, reapply TLH deltas onto upstream/main' target in entry gxtswg): as upstream drift grew (~21 ahead / ~59 behind at decision time), continuously rebasing fork deltas became too risky. New model: integrate upstream per release/tag or coherent feature CLUSTER (never per-commit) via explicit merge or squash-import PRs; track only exceptions/rejections + intake baselines in .upstream-ledger.jsonl (repo-root JSONL, like .gnosis/entries.jsonl); track deliberate fork deltas in docs/tlh-patch-inventory.md; git cherry/patch-id (and scripts/upstream-report.sh) are signal-only with the git DAG + ledger authoritative. Per-commit triage was considered and rejected as a pending-graveyard / dependency-skip risk. Full playbook: docs/UPSTREAM-SYNC.md. Supersedes gxtswg.

After 2026-07-12 investigation and contrarian review, Diego chose minimal-deletion scope for fork cleanup: (1) delete unshipped prompts/ directory (7 .md files, zero src/ references, not in npm manifest); (2) delete dead chain-expression parser cluster in src/slash/slash-commands.ts (buildChainExpressionSteps, parseChainExpression, hasGroupSyntax, parseGroupSegment, parseSingleTaskToken, findUnmatchedCloseParen, splitOnArrow, splitGroupTasks, validateInlineAcceptanceInput, loadInlineOutputSchema, and caller-less helpers/types/imports), verified zero callers in src/ before deletion; (3) delete parser-only test file test/unit/slash-chain-groups.test.ts. The 4 registered slash commands (subagents-doctor/models/profiles/check-profile), runSlashSubagent/requestSlashRun bridge flow, slash-bridge.ts, slash-live-state.ts, and prompt-template-bridge.ts are untouched. Broader cruft removal deferred: model-info.ts and acceptance machinery confirmed load-bearing (false-positive cruft candidates from initial audit); broader Tier1/Tier2 deletion rejected by contrarian review; post-v0.34 intake is the planned window for any broader cleanup.

2026-07-14 TLH trim phase 1 decisions by Diego: (1) tokens-first over maintainability when they conflict; (2) fail-closed static schema trim chosen over config-gated profile or subtractive filter after contrarian review proved Pi accepts unknown top-level params, TLH guard does not block worktree/clarify/share, and schema filtering strips TypeBox metadata — SubagentParams rewritten as a 19-param allowlist with additionalProperties:false and a closed action enum; initial checkpoint was 3,093B and final phase-1 schema is 3,068B; (3) chain mode dropped from the model-facing contract entirely because the architect sequences steps itself; (4) wait kept, with TLH issue #348 filed to adopt it in prompts; (5) bundled skill packaging/docs removed because TLH treats it as ambient-only and there is no runtime dependency; (6) rpc.ts must validate against the same fail-closed contract, with clarify injection removed, runId mapped to id, and dir dropped. Durable contract references live in README.md and docs/tlh-patch-inventory.md. Historical implementation handover: docs/HANDOVER-tlh-trim-phase1.md existed on branch tlh-trim-phase1 during delivery, but it is not a live source of truth.
author: Diego Petrucci <baulei@icloud.com>

text: On 2026-07-19 Diego clarified that the desired TLH end state is removal of the external pi-intercom package, not optimization of pi-intercom bridge mode. Acknowledgement-based suppression of the flat async completion was rejected as the target design because it would make rich intercom delivery authoritative. The migration should instead move required result, wake-up, control, and resume behavior into native pi-subagents paths in reviewable slices, retain compatibility implementation until native parity is validated, and remove the TLH pi-intercom installation only after that validation.
author: Diego Petrucci <baulei@icloud.com>

text: Phase-2a of the pi-subagents trim deliberately RESTORES the 'steer' action to the model-facing contract (schema enum + tool description), reversing phase-1 which had removed it. Reason: steer is the supported parent->child mid-run guidance channel, and it plus PR #64 (native owning-session async-completion delivery, the child->parent leg) are the two complementary NATIVE slices that replace the external pi-intercom broker. Do NOT naively re-remove steer as 'phase-1 dead surface': it was brought back on purpose for the intercom exit. Per gnosis vduuvz the pi-intercom install is removed from TLH only AFTER native parity is validated; until then keep compat plumbing (notably PI_SUBAGENT_BLOCKING_SUPERVISOR_REPLY_PATH, consumed only by pi-intercom) in the fork. Plain foreground children remain non-steerable by design (parent blocks on them).
author: Diego Petrucci <baulei@icloud.com>

text: On 2026-07-22 Diego explicitly reversed the earlier keep/defer decision for the TLH-hidden maintenance slash surfaces after confirming that current TLH code does not invoke them. Remove /subagents-models, /subagents-profiles, and /subagents-check-profile from the fork's registered/current-facing surface while preserving /subagents-doctor, /subagents-fleet, /subagent-cost, the programmatic models action, and underlying profile/model APIs. The bundled pi-subagents skill was already removed separately. TLH autocomplete/docs cleanup should accompany the eventual package release and pin bump, because the currently pinned package still registers the commands.
author: Diego Petrucci <baulei@icloud.com>
